title: metamagnolia macrophylla subsp. ashei
intro: Metamagnolia macrophylla subsp. ashei, a lesser-known subspecies of the Magnolia grandiflora, is a flowering plant native to the southeastern United States. The bark, leaves, and flowers of this plant have been traditionally used in herbal medicine for their antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. The medicinal properties of Metamagnolia macrophylla subsp. ashei are believed to provide several health benefits, including reducing fever, relieving respiratory issues, and acting as an antiseptic. This plant can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is also used in traditional folk medicine to treat various ailments, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
